get.summary: List ADT linkages for the caller's studio (admin)
get.description: Scoped to the caller's studio namespace (union_namespace ?? namespace). Returns identity columns only — no credential bytes exist (PRD §4.8.2).

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description:
Mirrors the existing admin RPC namespace path-param convention.
Service layer ignores it for authorization (linkage scope is the
studio derived from the caller's token, not this path), but the
value MUST equal AGS_NAMESPACE so the URL remains stable across
existing CORS / gateway routing.

delete.description: Idempotent re-unlink against an already soft-deleted row is a no-op success. Linkage absent for the caller's studio → NotFound (PRD §4.8). Best-effort calls ADT's DELETE /linkage in the same flow so the ADT-side flag and the local row drop together.

get.summary: List builds in an ADT linkage's namespace + game (admin)
get.description: Proxies adt.Client.ListBuilds keyed on the studio derived from the caller's token. Returns FailedPrecondition when ADT reports the linkage flag missing.

parameters.name: adtGameId
parameters.description: ADT game id under the linkage's namespace. Required.

get.summary: List games registered under an ADT linkage's namespace (admin)
get.description: Proxies adt.Client.ListGames keyed on the studio derived from the caller's token. Drives the create-playtest build-picker's top-level dropdown (STATUS_M5.md B12 + Addendum 2026-05-21). Returns FailedPrecondition when ADT reports the linkage flag missing.

post.description: Consumes the adt_link_pending row matching `state` (not expired); inserts the adt_linkage identity row with `adt_namespace` echoed by ADT on the callback URL. No outbound ADT call — tampering is self-defeating because the first downstream service-JWT call would 401 (PRD §4.8.2).

post.summary: Adopt an ADT-side linkage that has no local row (admin)
post.description: Operator-recovery surface for the 2026-05-21 orphan-flag bug: when ADT still carries a linkage flag but no local adt_linkage row exists, StartADTLink + the redirect dance fail with 409 / already_linked. RecoverADTLinkage probes ADT (ListGames) to confirm the orphan flag, then inserts the local row directly. No OAuth round-trip. AlreadyExists when a live row for (studio, adtNamespace) is already present; FailedPrecondition when ADT reports no flag for the pair; Unavailable on ADT transient errors.

Mints a 32-byte CSRF state, persists adt_link_pending, returns linkUrl that the admin UI redirects to. studio_namespace is derived server-side from the caller's token. No credential is exchanged (PRD §4.8.2).

post.summary: Approve a PENDING applicant (admin; reserve → fenced finalize per schema.md)
post.description: Re-approve on an already-APPROVED applicant returns the existing row (natural-key idempotency). Errors per docs/errors.md ApproveApplicant rows.
Re-reject returns the existing row (natural-key idempotency). rejection_reason is admin-visible (max 500 chars per schema.md).

post.summary: Re-send the approval DM for an applicant (admin; not idempotent)
post.description: No cooldown — double-click sends two DMs (PRD §5.4). Returns the updated Applicant row with refreshed DM fields.

get.summary: Report which ADT client kind was wired at boot + presence of every gate env var (admin)
get.description: Diagnostic surface for the 2026-05-21 silent-fallback bug: the bootapp gate that selects HTTP-backed vs in-memory ADT client requires ALL of AuthEnabled + ADT_BASE_URL + AGS_BASE_URL + AGS_IAM_CLIENT_ID + AGS_IAM_CLIENT_SECRET. When any one is empty the gate silently falls to the in-memory MemClient and UnlinkADT's ADT-side propagation becomes a no-op. This RPC returns the gate decision ("http" | "mem") plus a boolean presence flag for each env var so the operator can pinpoint the missing one without needing the boot log. Secret values are NEVER returned — only booleans.

get.summary: List playtests in a namespace (admin, unpaginated; 100-item cap)

post.description: STEAM_KEYS only in M1; distribution_model=AGS_CAMPAIGN returns Unimplemented until M2.

get.summary: Get a playtest by ID (admin)

patch.summary: Edit mutable fields on a playtest (admin)
patch.description: Editable: title, description, bannerImageUrl, platforms, startsAt, endsAt, ndaRequired, ndaText. Immutable fields → InvalidArgument.

get.summary: List announcement history for a playtest (admin)
get.description: Per-row status aggregated from announcement_recipient.dm_status: SENT (all sent), SENDING (any queued), PARTIAL (mix sent + failed), FAILED (all failed).

post.summary: Create a bulk DM broadcast for a playtest (admin)
post.description: Resolves recipients at call time (NOT a stored snapshot). Subject + message are PII-sensitive and are never written to audit JSONB or structured logs.

get.summary: List applicants for a playtest (admin; cursor pagination, page 50)
get.description: Order: createdAt DESC. Filters: status_filter (UNSPECIFIED → no filter), dm_failed_filter (true → only lastDmStatus='failed'). page_token is opaque; absent → start of stream. page_size 0 → server default (50).

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque cursor; absent → start of stream.

post.summary: Bulk-enqueue every failed-DM applicant for retry (admin; not idempotent)
post.description: Walks every applicant with last_dm_status=FAILED for the playtest and enqueues each through the same DM-queue path as approve, respecting the 10k cap and configured drain rate. Overflowed rows stay FAILED with last_dm_error='dm_queue_overflow' (PRD §5.5).

get.summary: List audit log entries for a playtest (admin; cursor pagination on (createdAt, id))
get.description: actor_filter='system' maps to actorUserId IS NULL per PRD §4.7. action_filter is exact-match on the action string. before_json / after_json carry the JSONB columns verbatim — the client renders the diff.

parameters.name: actorFilter
parameters.description:
Empty → no actor filter. 'system' maps to actorUserId IS NULL per
PRD §4.7 / §5.7.

parameters.name: actionFilter
parameters.description:
Empty → no action filter. Otherwise exact-match on the action
string (schema.md audit-action catalogue).

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque cursor; absent → start of stream.

get.summary: Inspect the code pool for a playtest (admin)
get.description: Returns aggregate counts plus the full code list including raw values — admin surfaces are exempt from the §6 log-redaction rule (PRD §5.7).

post.summary: Recover AGS_CAMPAIGN codes from the AGS side (admin; idempotent on UNIQUE(playtestId,value))
post.description: Fetch-only recovery for the case where AGS holds codes our DB never persisted. STEAM_KEYS playtests reject with FailedPrecondition.

post.summary: Generate additional AGS_CAMPAIGN codes (admin; not idempotent)
post.description: Each call generates a fresh batch via the AGS Campaign API. Per docs/ags-failure-modes.md the call is not transactional; partial fulfillment commits the codes received. STEAM_KEYS playtests reject with FailedPrecondition.

post.summary: Upload a CSV of STEAM_KEYS codes (admin; whole-file reject on any violation)
post.description: PRD §4.3: UTF-8, charset [A-Za-z0-9._-], 1–128 chars/code, file ≤10 MB, ≤50,000 codes, file-level + cross-row dedup. On any violation the response carries per-line rejection details and 0 codes are inserted.

get.summary: List participants with derived code-sent timestamps (admin; M5.C detail page)
get.description: Read joins applicant + the latest dm.sent audit row to derive code_sent_at for STEAM_KEYS / AGS_CAMPAIGN rows; ADT rows return NULL code_sent_at. Four ADT telemetry cache fields ship in the response shape but stay NULL/zero across M5.C.

parameters.name: statusFilter
parameters.description: Optional status filter; UNSPECIFIED → no filter.

post.summary: Create the survey for a playtest (admin; first-version only — second call returns AlreadyExists)
post.description: Natural-key on playtest_id. Server mints question UUIDs and multi-choice option UUIDs. Bounds: ≤50 questions, prompt ≤1,000 chars, multi-choice 2–20 options with label ≤200 chars (schema.md §"Survey entity spec").

patch.summary: Edit the survey for a playtest (admin; bumps version each call)
patch.description: Always creates a new Survey row with version = previous + 1. Question UUIDs are preserved for kept questions (client passes the existing id) and minted for new ones (id empty). Multi-choice option ids likewise — keeps histogram aggregation keys stable across edits per schema.md.

get.summary: List survey responses for a playtest (admin; cursor pagination on (submittedAt, id))
get.description: Default page_size 50, max 200. Optional survey_id_filter narrows to a single Survey version for per-version aggregate split.

parameters.name: surveyIdFilter
parameters.description:
Empty → all versions. Set to a Survey.id to narrow to a single
version for per-version aggregate split (PRD §5.6 cross-version
viewer).

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque cursor; absent → start of stream.

post.summary: Transition a playtest's status (admin; DRAFT→OPEN→CLOSED only)

get.summary: Worker leader-lease snapshot for the admin health banner (M4)
get.description: Returns one entry per registered background worker (reclaim_worker, window_worker). stale := now > expires_at + 2*tick_interval. Missing rows surface as lease_holder='' with stale=true so a never-ticked worker is unmissable. Reads leader_lease directly — no new table.

post.summary: Exchange a Discord OAuth authorization code for an AGS access token (unauthenticated)
post.description: Player runs Discord OAuth directly (Discord developer portal owns the redirect-URI allowlist). The resulting Discord authorization code is POSTed here; the backend authenticates with confidential AGS IAM credentials and calls /iam/v3/oauth/platforms/discord/token (platform-token grant). AGS auto-creates the Justice platform account on first call and returns AGS access + refresh tokens, which we forward verbatim. Replaces the auth-code federation flow attempted in STATUS.md M1 phase 9.2 — that flow's /iam/v3/oauth/token step always failed with invalid_grant in game namespaces because the auth-code path skips Justice-platform-account creation.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
ExchangeDiscordCodeRequest carries a Discord OAuth authorization code
for the backend to exchange against AGS IAM's platform-token grant.
The player obtained the code by driving discord.com/oauth2/authorize
directly — AGS IAM is not involved until this RPC. The redirect_uri
MUST byte-exactly match the value the player sent to Discord; AGS
passes it through to Discord, which re-validates.

get.summary: Get the authenticated caller's identity (player)
get.description: Returns the caller's AGS user id plus the best-effort Discord handle resolved via the same bot-token lookup the signup flow uses. discord_handle is empty when the caller is not Discord-federated or the lookup fails — the field is informational; callers must not treat it as authoritative identity.

get.summary: Get the ADT build download URL for the caller (authenticated player; APPROVED applicants only)
get.description: FailedPrecondition for non-ADT playtests. URL re-minted on every call so the player always sees a fresh (possibly per-applicant) URL.

get.summary: Get the granted code for the caller (authenticated player; APPROVED applicants only)
get.description: NotFound for any soft-deleted playtest regardless of applicant state (PRD §5.1 / errors.md). FailedPrecondition for ADT playtests — use GetADTDownloadInfo.

get.summary: Get the current survey for a playtest (authenticated player)
get.description: Returns the version pointed at by Playtest.surveyId. NotFound when the playtest has no survey.

post.summary: Submit a one-shot survey response (authenticated player; APPROVED + NDA-current applicants only)
post.description: Natural-key on (playtestId, userId). Second submit returns gRPC AlreadyExists with empty body per errors.md row 31. The server records against the survey_id the client submitted — a concurrent EditSurvey does not invalidate an in-flight submit (PRD §5.6).

post.summary: Accept the current NDA for a playtest (authenticated player)
post.description: Idempotent on (userId, playtestId, ndaVersionHash) per PRD §4.7. Backend recomputes the current hash from the live playtest row; second accept on the same tuple returns the existing NDAAcceptance, no error.

get.summary: Get a playtest by slug (authenticated player)
get.description: Returns the player-visible field set including NDA text and currentNdaVersionHash.

get.summary: Get the caller's own applicant row for a playtest

post.summary: Sign up for a playtest (authenticated player; idempotent on (playtestId, userId))

get.summary: Get the public client config (unauthenticated)
get.description: Returns environment-derived client config that both the admin and player frontends need to construct cross-app URLs. player_base_url is the public origin of the player Svelte bundle (from backend env PLAYER_BASE_URL); empty string when unset.

get.summary: Get a playtest by slug (unauthenticated)
get.description: Returns the public field subset for an OPEN playtest. NotFound for DRAFT, CLOSED, or soft-deleted.

state.description: CSRF nonce echoed by ADT on the redirect-back URL.

adtNamespace.description:
ADT namespace ADT echoed on the redirect-back URL. Required —
missing/empty → InvalidArgument byte-exact (errors.md).

autoApprove.description:
PRD §5.4 auto-approve (M5.A). auto_approve_limit must be set
(1..100,000) when auto_approve is true; reject byte-exact per
docs/errors.md.

adtNamespace.description:
ADT distribution (M5.B). Required when distribution_model = ADT;
rejected when set on STEAM_KEYS / AGS_CAMPAIGN. The linkage row
covering (studio_namespace, adt_namespace) must already exist —
CompleteADTLink first. adt_build_id is verified against
adt.Client.ListBuilds(adt_namespace, adt_game_id) at create-time.

questions.description:
Ordered question array. Server mints UUIDs for every question +
option (any client-supplied id is ignored on Create — see EditSurvey
for the preserve-on-edit path). Bounds enforced server-side per
schema.md.

autoApprove.description:
PRD §5.4 auto-approve (M5.A). Both fields are mutable mid-playtest
— operators tune the cap while signups are flowing in.

adtFallbackDownloadUrl.description:
ADT distribution (M5.B). adt_namespace / adt_game_id / adt_build_id
are immutable post-create (mirrors ags_item_id); only the static
fallback URL is editable so operators can repoint without
recreating the playtest.

PlaytesthubServiceEditPlaytestBody.description:
EditPlaytestRequest mutable-field whitelist tracks PRD §5.1 L146.
Immutable fields (slug, namespace path params excluded, status,
distribution_model, initial_code_quantity, ags_*, timestamps) are
deliberately absent — clients that send them on the JSON body still have
them rejected at the handler layer.

questions.description:
Ordered question array. For each entry: `id` non-empty → preserve
(must match an existing question's id on the current version);
`id` empty → mint a new UUID. Same rule applies to MultiChoiceOption.id.
The server bumps version each call.

adtNamespace.description:
ADT namespace whose orphan flag (present on ADT's side, absent
locally) the caller wants to adopt. Required.

surveyId.description:
The Survey.id (version) the client fetched and is submitting
against. Pinned to defeat mid-fill version race per PRD §5.6.

csvContent.description: CSV file contents. UTF-8, ≤10 MB. Server-side validation per PRD §4.3.

filename.description:
Original filename echoed into the code.upload / code.upload_rejected
audit rows (schema.md §"AuditLog — `action` enum"). Optional —
server treats absent as "" and the audit row records the empty
string.

platform.description:
Platform — the ADT-reported target ("windows", "linux", ...). Empty
when ADT does not surface it. Wire-added 2026-05-20 alongside the
ADT-eng API spec resolution.

v1ADTBuild.description:
ADTBuild is the minimum build row the admin UI / pth CLI needs to
drive the build picker. Mirrors pkg/adt.Build but rendered as proto.

v1ADTGame.description:
ADTGame is the minimum game row the admin UI / pth CLI needs to drive
the create-playtest build-picker's top-level dropdown. Mirrors
pkg/adt.Game (STATUS_M5.md "Addendum 2026-05-21 — games-list endpoint").

v1ADTLinkage.description:
ADTLinkage mirrors the adt_linkage row (schema.md §"adt_linkage table"
/ PRD §4.8.1). Identity columns only — no credential payload exists
to leak (PRD §4.8.2). `deleted_at` is unset on live rows.

v1Announcement.description:
Announcement — one row per admin-authored broadcast. Subject + message
are PII-sensitive (never logged / audited).

v1AnnouncementSendToFilter.description:
M5.C — announcement send-to filter (PRD §5.4 "Bulk announcements").
Resolved at call time, NOT a stored snapshot.

v1AnnouncementStatus.description:
M5.C — announcement aggregate status computed at read time from
announcement_recipient.dm_status (PRD §5.4 "Bulk announcements" + schema.md).

autoApproved.description:
PRD §5.4 auto-approve (M5.A). True when this applicant was
approved via the auto-approve signup chain rather than a manual
ApproveApplicant click. Used for cap accounting.

surveyResponseSubmittedAt.description:
Player-visible. Set when the caller has already submitted a survey
response for this playtest (PRD §5.6 one-shot). Powers the
post-submit "Feedback submitted ✓" affordance on the Pending page so
the client can flip CTA state without an extra round-trip. Unset
when no response exists or surveys aren't configured.

v1Applicant.description:
Applicant is the full admin-visible entity (schema.md §"Applicant entity").
Player-visible responses carry the same message with restricted fields
populated — the handler layer enforces the visibility split (schema.md
L86–88). Player-visible: status, granted_code_id (presence), approved_at,
nda_version_hash.

v1AuditLogEntry.description:
AuditLogEntry — one row from the AuditLog table (schema.md §"AuditLog
table"). before_json / after_json carry the JSONB payload columns
serialized as opaque JSON strings — the client renders the diff and
the wire shape stays stable as schema.md grows new payload variants.
actor_user_id absent ⇄ actorUserId IS NULL (system-emitted event).

v1Code.description:
Code — full admin-visible pool entity (schema.md §"Code table").
Player-facing surfaces (GetGrantedCode) return the bare code value
instead of the entity.

v1CodePoolStats.description:
CodePoolStats — aggregate counts. The admin UI low-pool banner
(≤10% remaining) is computed off these.

v1CodeState.description:
CodeState — per-code state machine for the Code pool (PRD §5.5,
schema.md §"Code table"). Pool-only grant (no external entitlement
in MVP).

redirectUri.description:
The same redirect_uri the player sent to discord.com/oauth2/authorize.
Discord rejects byte-mismatches with invalid_grant.

v1ExchangeDiscordCodeRequest.description:
ExchangeDiscordCodeRequest carries a Discord OAuth authorization code
for the backend to exchange against AGS IAM's platform-token grant.
The player obtained the code by driving discord.com/oauth2/authorize
directly — AGS IAM is not involved until this RPC. The redirect_uri
MUST byte-exactly match the value the player sent to Discord; AGS
passes it through to Discord, which re-validates.

accessToken.description:
AGS IAM access token (JWT) for the federated user. Player stores in
sessionStorage and attaches as Authorization: Bearer to player RPCs.

refreshToken.description: AGS IAM refresh token (opaque). Forwarded as-is for future rotation.

expiresIn.description: Lifetime of access_token in seconds, per AGS IAM response.

tokenType.description: Always "Bearer" today; carried verbatim for forward-compat.

adtClientKind.description:
Which adt.Client implementation the bootapp wired:
  "http" — adt.NewHTTPClient (live ADT calls).
  "mem"  — adt.NewMemClient (no-op for ADT-side propagation).

adtBaseUrlSet.description: True when ADT_BASE_URL was non-empty at boot.

agsBaseUrlSet.description: True when AGS_BASE_URL was non-empty at boot.

agsIamClientIdSet.description: True when AGS_IAM_CLIENT_ID was non-empty at boot.

agsIamClientSecretSet.description: True when AGS_IAM_CLIENT_SECRET was non-empty at boot.

v1GetADTClientDiagnosticsResponse.description:
GetADTClientDiagnosticsResponse — booleans only. Values for the AGS
IAM client id / secret are NEVER returned; only their presence
(non-empty at boot) is exposed.

urls.description:
Resolved download URLs in ADT's original order. Issued via
adt.Client.IssueDownloadURL when ADT can mint URLs (one per build
asset — single-file builds → one element, multi-asset builds →
many); falls back to a single-element list containing the
playtest's static adtFallbackDownloadUrl when ADT 4xx/5xx but the
linkage row is still present.

expiresAt.description: Optional expiry; zero when ADT does not surface one.

codes.description:
Raw code values included — admin surfaces are exempt from the §6
log-redaction rule (PRD §5.7).

value.description:
Raw redemption value. For STEAM_KEYS this is the Steam key; for
AGS_CAMPAIGN it is the AGS-generated alphanumeric code. The player
app renders model-specific instructions off distribution_model.

playerBaseUrl.description:
Public origin of the player Svelte bundle (from backend env
PLAYER_BASE_URL). Empty string when unset — callers SHOULD treat
empty as "player URL not configured" and surface that to the user
rather than falling back silently.

v1MultiChoiceOption.description:
MultiChoiceOption — one entry on a multi_choice question. `id` is
server-minted on first save and preserved across EditSurvey version
bumps so histogram aggregation keys stay stable (schema.md §"Survey
entity spec"). On EditSurvey, clients pass the existing `id` for
kept options and leave it empty for new options.

v1NDAAcceptance.description:
NDAAcceptance — append-only acceptance row (schema.md §"NDA"). PK is
(user_id, playtest_id, nda_version_hash); a re-accept on the same
tuple is a no-op that returns the existing row.

codeSentAt.description:
STEAM_KEYS / AGS_CAMPAIGN only — derived at read time from the
latest dm.sent audit row (PRD §5.4 "Code Sent Date — derived field").
ADT rows leave this NULL; the analogue (Download Date) is deferred to M6.

adtDownloadAt.description:
Four dormant ADT telemetry cache fields (M5.C → M6 forward-compat).
All are NULL / 0 across M5.C.

v1ParticipantRow.description:
ParticipantRow — one row per applicant for the M5.C Participants tab.
Cache-only read; the four ADT telemetry fields ship dormant across
M5.C (always NULL / 0 — M6 fills them).

v1PlayerPlaytest.description:
PlayerPlaytest is the field set returned to authenticated player callers
(GetPlaytestForPlayer). Superset of PublicPlaytest plus NDA + distribution
+ status fields needed for the player flow.

autoApprove.description:
auto_approve / auto_approve_limit — PRD §5.4 auto-approve (M5.A).
When auto_approve is true, signup chains into the approve path up
to auto_approve_limit applicants. auto_approve_limit MUST be set
when auto_approve is true (bounded 1..100,000, DB-enforced).

adtNamespace.description:
ADT distribution fields (PRD §5.1 / §4.8, M5.B). Populated when
distribution_model = DISTRIBUTION_MODEL_ADT; all three identifiers
immutable post-create. adt_fallback_download_url is the static
per-playtest URL used when ADT can't mint a per-applicant URL.

v1Playtest.description: Playtest is the full admin-visible entity. Field semantics: PRD §5.1.

v1PublicPlaytest.description:
PublicPlaytest is the restricted field set exposed to unauthenticated
callers on GetPublicPlaytest (PRD §5.2).

enqueued.description: Number of applicants successfully enqueued onto the DM queue.

linkUrl.description:
Full URL the admin UI assigns to window.location.href to begin the
ADT-side linking dance. Carries the redirect_uri + state +
studio_namespace query params (PRD §4.8.2).

state.description:
The opaque CSRF nonce. Admin UI keeps it (and ADT round-trips it
verbatim) so CompleteADTLink can match the adt_link_pending row.

expiresAt.description:
Expiry of the adt_link_pending row, in seconds-from-epoch. Surfaced
so a CLI driver can decide whether to retry before the row sweeps.

v1Survey.description:
Survey — one row per (playtest, version). Editing a survey writes
a new row with version = previous + 1; the prior versions are
retained so SurveyResponse rows can be aggregated per-version
(PRD §5.6).

v1SurveyMultiChoiceAnswer.description:
SurveyAnswer — one entry per answered question on a SurveyResponse.
The oneof keeps text, rating, and multi-choice payloads typed and
mutually exclusive. multi_choice carries either one option_id
(radio) or many (checkbox) — matched to the question's
allow_multiple flag at submit time.

v1SurveyQuestion.description:
SurveyQuestion — one entry in a Survey's ordered question array.
`id` server-minted on first save; preserved across edits for kept
questions (clients pass it back), empty for new questions.
`options` populated only for SURVEY_QUESTION_TYPE_MULTI_CHOICE; the
server rejects on save if outside 2–20 entries. `allow_multiple`
distinguishes single-select (radio) from multi-select (checkbox)
rendering on the player side.

v1SurveyQuestionType.description:
SurveyQuestionType — discriminator for the typed-question shape.
`text` (free-text textarea), `rating` (fixed 1–5), `multi_choice`
(2–20 labelled options).

v1SurveyResponse.description:
SurveyResponse — one row per (playtest, user). UNIQUE(playtestId,
userId) at the DB level enforces one-shot semantics regardless of
survey version bumps (schema.md §"Survey entity spec"). `survey_id`
pins the Survey row (version) the client fetched and submitted
against.

added.description:
Net new rows persisted by this sync (deduped against existing
UNIQUE(playtest_id, value) constraint).

Codes actually inserted into the pool. May be < requested on
partial fulfillment per docs/ags-failure-modes.md.

value.description:
Echo of the offending value when relevant (e.g. duplicate). Empty
for whole-file failures (non-UTF-8, file-size, row-count caps).

v1UploadCodesRejection.description:
UploadCodesRejection — one entry per offending CSV row when the
upload is rejected wholesale (PRD §4.3 / errors.md UploadCodes
"bounds / charset / duplicate violation"). line_number is 1-based.

inserted.description:
Number of codes inserted. 0 when rejections is non-empty (whole-
file reject — no partial commits).

userId.description:
AGS user id in dashless form (matches the wire shape the rest of
the player surface uses — pkg/agsid.Format).

discordHandle.description:
Best-effort human-readable Discord handle (e.g. "shadowrealm#4421").
Empty when the caller is not Discord-federated, the Discord bot
client is unconfigured, or the lookup fails. Informational — never
treat as identity, never log alongside NDA / survey content.

discordId.description:
Discord snowflake when available. Empty otherwise. Useful for the
frontend to badge the account without round-tripping through AGS.

v1WorkerHealthEntry.description:
WorkerHealthEntry mirrors one row of the leader_lease table with the
addition of a server-computed `stale` flag (now > expires_at +
2*tick_interval per STATUS_M4.md phase 5). Workers that have never
elected a leader return with lease_holder="" and stale=true so the
admin banner makes a never-ticked worker unmissable.
